reviews.review_text:
Bought R.E.P.O. thinking it was a co-op horror game.
What I got instead was:
a piano that weighs approximately the same as the moon
a friend who dropped a priceless ceramic vase down the stairs immediately
a monster that only activates when someone says "is it safe"
and a cart we lost in the first two minutes and never spoke of again.
The objective is simple. Carry valuable items carefully to extraction. Do not break them. We have never once done this.
The physics system is a masterpiece of human suffering:
"Okay, careful, careful—"
The vase flies sideways into a wall.
It was worth $4,000.
We needed $4,000.
Dave is no longer allowed to touch things.
The proximity voice chat adds incredible immersion. You can hear your friends whispering in real time as you creep through a dark mansion. You can also hear the exact moment someone starts screaming because a monster that looks like a rejected Pixar villain walked through a door at 2% speed and somehow this is the most terrifying experience of our collective lives.
The monster AI is elegantly simple. It hears noise. We make noise. This is our entire relationship with death.
The upgrade shop offers meaningful progression. We bought better lungs. We needed better lungs because we keep screaming. We are still screaming. We just have more health while we do it.
10/10.
We never hit quota.
We're going back in.
Someone find the cart.
You can grab literal baby by it's neck and smash it into the ground until it explodes then you can sell it's soul  for money. Masterpiece. Never Change.

I do like REPO, but there are so many frustrating things in this game that have ruined me enjoying this game as much as I'd want to enjoy it. There IS a good game here, but I don't see many people pointing out the MANY negative or frustrating aspects of this game, so I wanted to point some of these out and I hope the developers see this so the game can be the best that it can be.

Firstly let me talk about maybe my biggest issue, the Huntsman AI. I don't know why many people think he is ok, but I've heard it's worse on the host's end and I host often so maybe that's why I have such an Issue with it and others don't. I have had MANY times where he shoots me either across an entire room where I could not hope to react and get one tapped, or he shoots me when ANOTHER sound source made him trigger and he shoots me anyway. I've had times where I tap a object on the ground to bait his shot, and instead of shooting the object far away from me he shoots me instead. This makes absolutely no sense and I personally think he should not work this way, he should always shoot the sound source not the nearest player like how he does at times. I suggest taking inspiration from the Eyeless Dogs from Lethal Company, just make him not instantly shoot at the first sound he hears and make him have an audio cue where players can be aware they are making too much noise like a sound of him grumbling or him going "huh", and if they continue he then shoots them. I also think his idle sounds should play more often and have more range so you can hear him before you get in his range so less unfair deaths where you didn't even know he was there at the edge of your vision occur. I think in general the monsters in this game are too quiet/don't have idle sounds that are good sound cues. Besides some exceptions like the Loom/Robe, them being quiet is part of their design.

This is a little less important but, WHY DO SOME COSMETICS GIVE YOU ONE OUT OF A PAIR?! I genuinely don't understand why they did this, because I've had so many times where I get a cosmetic that looks like I'd use it... IF ONLY I GOT THE PAIR AND NOT ONE FOR A SINGLE SHOE/LEG. Some cosmetics do look cool asymmetrically, but (in my opinion) most don't and this just makes cosmetics grindy for the ones you want because the chances of getting the other one in the pair are low. I just don't see any positives to it being this way because if you wanted to just use one, you could just equip one out of the two if you got the cosmetic in a pair anyway. But I do like everything else about the cosmetic system they did a good job on how they work in every other way. I just really disagree with it working like this with pairs of cosmetics.

Back to another big issue (again in my opinion). The physics system is WAY to janky in some areas, mostly with big items. Big items take damage for absolutely no reason very often. Sometimes you'll just touch it to check the value... BOOM -$2,377 for no reason. Sometimes you'll slide it across the ground gently... BOOM -$5,078 for no reason. Sometimes you rotate it slightly... BOOM -$3,980 for no reason. The big items really need to be less janky because a lot of the time they quickly become not worth it because it randomly took damage from sliding on a carpeted floor. When moving big items is a main game mechanic, it should be polished.
 Also I'm not sure whether to call this a physics issue or not but, you also should not be able to hit yourself with a melee weapon so easily. Many times I've needed a melee weapon quickly and hit myself while pulling it out, getting me killed. 

I also have an issue with the early levels having a tier 3 enemy like a Trudge or Head, which would not be an issue if the levels had more paths. What often happens (especially when I played solo with the Trudge) is the monster blocks and camps where you need to go and they waste your time, and because its early game (especially bad on the first level) you can't deal with these guys and just have to wait 4-8min just for them to leave. I think this is worse with the Trudge because he is so slow and takes forever to leave and it's difficult to run past him without getting one shot. In the later levels this is not as bad because you and the monsters have more paths to go, but in early game it really can be frustrating and slow down the game more than it should. I would just make the early levels have more pathways to counter this issue. Also because I'm talking about the Trudge, I think he should not be able to kill you through walls and suck you in through walls either. But this could be a bug and not an intentional gameplay mechanic. I've also been killed by the Cleanup Crew (UH OH guy) when I was enclosed in a safe through the wall from the head explosion.

Another thing is the weapon spread from some ranged weapons (mostly the handgun and similar weapons). Some weapons have insane bullet spread (even after they buffed it). They already have limited ammo, why do I have to be in hugging distance to make sure I don't miss? It makes the weapons feel a tad underpowered.
 
The stamina system early game is a pain to deal with as well, mostly the recovery rate. The amount of stamina you get early game is barely enough to survive and if you aren't in a good spot, don't have full stamina or don't hide perfectly; you just die to some enemies without a chance(head, duck, Robe etc.). You can upgrade both of these aspects, but in a game where you're playing with multiple people you don't get enough till very late because everyone needs it. I think they should AT LEAST make stamina recovery faster with no upgrades.

But that's all. I do enjoy this game, but I don't see people talking about these issues, (even though some can be seen as nitpicks) some can kill the fun of the game (Huntsman). It's ok to criticize a game, it can still be good. It helps the game be the best it can be. For anyone who read this, thank you for reading my rant/critique. I hope the developers take this feedback because there is a really good game here held back by some frustration, and unfair AI, mechanics or bugs.
Thank you for reading!
Just as good as Lethal Company, but different enough to be its own thing. Very fun, cool monsters and now with customisation!
10/10 game. Huntsman is good at falling down the stairs

there should be a difference in single player monsters and muliplayer monsters...some monsters you simply can not deal with solo! as for the items to extract with as well there should be all smallers items for a solo player to put in the cart to extract with, i keep finding bigger items that just keep breaking playing solo no matter how carefull i am

the monsters chasing me in dark hallways got me so hard that im bussing my boy load at them when they catch me, had me pushing so much p that i couldnt hold my balls correctly almost felt like i had a tumor in my sack. good game tho

I love this game. It’s goofy and entertaining. It’s strategic but doesn’t take itself too seriously. My whole family enjoys both playing and watching people play it (especially when Grian, Geminitay, ImpulseSV, and Skizz play it). It has elements which are similar to Lethal Company, but I appreciate the differences between the games more than anything. I can’t play Lethal because it’s just a bit too creepy/spooky for me with clunky characters. Another big difference is the way you can “go out”. It’s very amusing to knock the other players off a platform to become “king of the losers”. I’ve seen people get out their frustration with this fun mechanic. 

And here’s where my suggestion comes into play: If there is a loser island face off, surely there should be a form of “winner face-off”. After who-knows-how-many levels (perhaps there could be a set goal in the settings?), the players could emerge onto that platform, not as losers, but as winners. They can sip tea (like eating a consumable), then take out their angst for certain monsters by placing toy-sized versions of them on an extraction table that smashes them. All of the while, credits can roll. And here’s another fun aspect to the idea. If they want, they can allow their teamwork to shine and all become REPO winners, or they can take each other out as with the other ending and become “king of the winners”. It could be an even better tag than losing because they reached a goal. So satisfying if you ask me. 

Reasons I think many people would enjoy an ending similar to this outline:
I love setting a goal and reaching it. I love smashing and venting frustration on the actual monsters (hence the smashing of those silly things in an after-party). I like the idea of a celebration tea, whether it be a teacup, cake, oil can, whatever the lore would suggest. And then the opportunity to all be winners because it’s truly teamwork that gets many people through this game, is fun to me; of course, if they would rather be king of the hill, that’s their prerogative. If they can manage to get through the ongoing credits without taking out their friends, then they really deserve that cute crown. 

Yep. Those are my two cents. Love the game, but I’d prefer reaching a point where I can log off for the night feeling like I accomplished something, rather than have a bit of irk that we “lost” again. 
Edit: I do love some of the new features to become King of the losers.

P.S. Once at the winner island there could even be a red button called Hard Mode which uses a trap door to send players back to the truck with their [previously purchased] stuff, because people who reach the winner island are likely to be more confident and eager for another challenge.

somehow in a group of 5 ppl i always end up the last one standing in museum of human arts and i think thats funny
10/10 game to play with friends for hours on end, always have a blast

This game isn't about stealth. It's about hiding in a corner for frustrating amounts of time while slow creatures lumber around right where you need to be. It's a game about enemies getting cheep shots in before you know they are there. It's a game about tantalizingly damageable enemies that kill you far to easily if you don't swing your mouse just right when you go to hit them. It's a game that punishes you when you try to do anything other than hide. In short, everything about this game is frustrating for me to the extreme. What's worse, is according to my friend group, it's a good game, and I am fun to play with. So, I'm still playing the master class of a rage gripping slog of a game, ripping my hair out and begging for them to find another game to play. I can't really say it's a bad game, but I can say it is not for me.
This game is super fun and cool and you can laugh with your friends with one of the best pranks.
this game is perfect i hope they gonna add 1 more type of transport like a bigger cargo car or a helicopter
this game has simultaneously ruined and beautifully fostered my relationships with everyone i play it with

A fun little loot collection physics horror game. It's in the style of Lethal Company but I honestly think this game is superior because you get a decent fighting chance against the monsters and you don't instantly die to everything. The full profit of your hauls goes to the shopping experience immediately after. One downside of this game is that in singleplayer the game can grow long and tedious.

There are also 540 cosmetics to collect so good luck with that, but honestly they went overboard with this amount as there are a ton of low tier cosmetics you'll never use, and you typically only get one of each (e.g. 1 arm, not the other) while cosmetic boxes are fairly rare. Another thing I'm hoping for at the time of writing is some more official maps and a little bit of bug fixing as some of the physics can be frustrating, like items taking damage when you let go even though they're completely stationary.
Great game to kill an evening with friends. But in order to have fun you need funny friends.
the game is both fun alone and with friends but mostly friends one thing i dislike though is how some monsters are very overpowered or overall just downright horrible

You're going to have hours of fun with your friends.
Game mechanics are interesting to learn solo. (there's movement tech too)
I already liked the game before it, but the cosmetics update has kept me craving this game once in a while.
Questionably low timeout threshold makes it unplayable on somewhat unstable connections. No other game has issues with this.

One of the best "friend games" out there.

Very polished, very scary, very funny.

Each monster is unique and has a different way to play around it, I sure hope they keep adding more. My favorite so far is The Loom.

I did play a bit of solo but it's a completely different experience, I don't think I'd recommend it if you don't like to lose a lot, but it can be done. (My highest level solo is 14)

Devs are very transparent, make funny and cool update videos which I personally really like. And they even highlight community mods!

The game recently introduced the cosmetic update which is really good, you get cosmetics completely for free just for playing, there's a lot of options to choose from and you can even color separate parts of your outfit. 

Also you can save cosmetic sets that you created and wear them whenever you want. [b]Sea of Thieves could never.[/b]
This is one of THEE if not the best and one of the most fun "friend slop" game there is! the cosmetic update made the game so much more fun and feel rewarding.

I know I am probably in the minority but I feel like this game is unplayable. I am not a great gamer and I just want to have a fun experience with my friends. I want a challenging yet rewarding game. I feel like I cannot play this games without mods. but the mods I play with break the game and also make the game unplayable. 

We just played two games and both games had the balloon boy and the head bomb dude and the scale doesn't ramp up 0-20, 20-40. 40-60 it goes from 100 to a thousand. Then you have like 8 options now in the store and you can reroll for 5k? that's insane. 

Can we get some casual modes? can I not have to worry about not  being able to buy upgrades or be able to kill any monsters without being a god level player? I want to be able for each player to at least be able to purchase one upgrade per round. I don't think that's too much to ask for. For those who love the game as is, that's fantastic I don't want to change their experience.  I'm sorry in theory its a great game but dayum its so frustrating to be such a struggle. Its not for the casual players its for hardcore fans in my opinion. 

I am mid game and im rambling, please help

Feel like it's fair for me to give this a review now. When I first heard of the game I didn't think anything of it cause I never looked into it. Thought it was going to be some flash in the pan ripoff banking on the success of Lethal Company. But man was I wrong. The game is the perfect game to play with friends.  The support for the game has been amazing. Seeing the devs upload videos and keep us in the loop with what's coming is great. 
Now the mod support! Fantastic, my friends and I play it in VR, now while not officially supported the devs recognize it and approve of it.  Playing this game in VR is so good, the sense of scale of the world and the monsters is completely different than playing flat.

A brief recommendation, but one I should make nonetheless. Of all the games belonging to the "do stupid stuff with friends in procedurally generated environments, with focus on replayability" games (I refuse to acknowledge the "friendslop" term), R.E.P.O is the one I've had the most fun with and the most polished.

The game is rather cheap, the physics-based gameplay is fun and complements perfectly the game's silly nature, resulting in a lot of funny moments in which I found myself recording a lot of clips. But besides being funny, there's some depth to the gameplay here: You can hide, run, resource managing such as deciding to spend items to eliminate threats or boost your survivability, coordinate with friends to haul heavy objects or deal with foes, and purchase upgrades that last the whole run and each strengthens a different aspect of your character. It's not perfect, but honestly it's more than enough for what it tries to do and for the asking price.

The recent "Cosmetic Update" is surprisingly in-depth, way beyond what I've been expecting. I'm sure that'll also add to the game's replayability. Props for the team for all the effort that went into making the new cosmetic customization systems..

Also, it's worth mentioning that you can play with up to 6 players, and it's gloriously chaotic.

Really good! Its really fun, and they're still updating it with lots of new fun stuff every few months or so. Though if you don't have a mic, people can be really rude, but its still fun either way. And they have a chat system. That's how me and my friends play, because we can't use mics.

If you're looking for a game that can make you laugh and scream at the same time, you just found it. It's funnier and scarier with friends around. Get serious on the job, troll each other, chase monsters to either fight or die, it's up to you. But it's definitely the best of both worlds and worth a try!

I always shied away from 'friendslop' games because I never found them engaging enough or way too much of a time commitment to wrangle together a group to sustainably play for hours.
I found REPO extremely fun and solved a lot of the problems I feel like a lot of co-op games post Lethal Company & Peak ran into.

There's really no barrier for entry for this game, it's extremely easy to pick up and learn on the spot. I and a few other friends skipped the tutorial and were able to get the hang of it quickly w/ help from more experienced friends.
I also felt like playing with friends who have 30+ hrs in the game didn't diminish my own gameplay experience as a newcomer.

The game is balances scary and goofy in a really fun way, and while I think the experience could come down to making or breaking on who you play with, having an active but determined group makes for the best experiences. 
It's all fun and games until you're the newbie whos the last one alive and one checkpoint still needs to be fulfilled. Scariest moment of my entire life, I hope the Huntsman dies in a fire.

REPO is definitely worth it if you got friends to play with, I know I'm a bit late on the hype train but even now I still had a great time :)) It's super fun and engaging and terrifying at the right moments. A perfect co-op game that balances everyone's skill level while still allowing it to be fun & terrifying.

Such a fun game to play with your friends. The new cosmetics add solid extra long term reason to play. Controls are smooth and gameplay is fun and chaotic. Sometimes the maps seem just totally unfair for the first missions when you get the "right" monster combos. Overall game is a blast though with friends.

an accurate representation of corporate America: Even when everybody is either dead or horribly mutated, taxes find a way to persist. also ran very well on my 15 year-old computer with no crashes or lag-spikes!
very fun, challenging levels, and eerienes, best way to play it with friends
It's a great game, a fun horror movie where you can enjoy spending time with your friends. I thank the developers for releasing new updates and following the game.
The eye logic is genius, and the free update... man the devs love their game i can tell.

Words cannot really express how much fun this game is when you play with friends. The excitement that comes with taking risks, combined with voice communications has allowed me to enjoy some hilarious moments with people I know in such a short amount of time.

If you have friends and you intend to play a team-based game with horror elements, combined with risk and reward, this is for you.

This was one of those games which I didn't think much of before I actually played the game with a couple of my friends. Now, those sessions have turned into insane public lobbies which are pretty fun if you can control the chaos around you! Other than that, the game is really fun and I hope the developers invent more crazy ways to aid or hinder the Semibots.

9/10. love this game but developers PLEASE i'm begging you to add an option of copying the world with progress or an ability to leave the game early before you die like they have it in lethal company. because dying with so much progress, losing it and having to start it all over again makes me wanna break my laptop and not play again. other than that, great game :)

This game was very fun to play with my siblings! If you're up for horror games that include high quality graphics like this, Repo is the game for you. I'm a huge horror fan myself, and this game beat the horror score.

My friend and i bought this game and have put almost 20 hrs into it together. The game is super fun and great with friends and really easy to be goofy on. i think the game can get a little repetitive since there is only like 5 maps, but the game receives pretty frequent updates so i think it'll be perfectly fine for casual players, or someone just wanting a game to have fun with friends for only $10.

By far the best horror co-op game I've played. R.E.P.O. has a ton of creative and fun gameplay mechanics to keep you engaged. Every run plays differently due to the randomness the game throws at you. It never fails to offer an amazing combination of scary, hilarious, challenging and rewarding moments.

Clearly made by passionate and talented devs. Can't wait to see how this game evolves.
Love the game. Lots of modders and sound board users ruin the fun.

Great co-op game to play with friends or find new friends. Silly and fun and you can get good at it relatively quickly.

All I will say it it does get repetitive after abt 30-50 hours. I've gone through a few phases with it and with my friends. At a certain point, you can start to use mods, but the mods aren't that good either.
it is VERY fun! I play it every day. If your mic doesnt work, theres text to speech! if you dont have friends, you can play single player! (or public lobbies!)

Fun game to play with friends.
Although the lag is a bit much sometimes when playing with people overseas. 
The physics should be client-side.

This game is very fun :) the creators did very good with making this game scary and fun. i love playing it with my friends and it is very funny. we can be collecting items and trying not to break them, or being chased by monsters and screaming our lungs out. very fun game
always better to play with friends ofc, but its an amazing game

R.E.P.O is one of the funniest horror co-op games you can play with friends. The chaos, teamwork, and funny moments make every match entertaining and memorable. But playing solo is a completely different experience it becomes extremely difficult and honestly not that enjoyable alone because of how punishing the game can be.

The game has a lot of potential, but it still needs more updates, content, and balancing improvements to keep things fresh. In my opinion, the maps would also feel much better if they were more open spaced instead of so tight and restrictive. Bigger environments could make exploration and gameplay more exciting.

Overall, R.E.P.O is a super fun game with friends and has amazing potential for the future if the developers continue improving it. <3
This game was the most fun I have had on a horror game and the most funniest
to many one shot monsters but very funny when u see ur friend walk backwards into a hole as ur other friend pets a dog so they dont die

Randomly addictive and the existence of cosmetics now makes this that much better, game's super fun with friends, runs on a toaster, is cheap with a ♥♥♥♥ ton of replayability and its still getting updates ! Far more than worth its bucks, this is THE friendslop. Also still very entertaining to play solo !
The integration of voice in this game is fun! They also give the dead some way to interact which is something a lot of similar games seem to miss.

Very fun game. It never really gets boring just because there are so many items and things to do. Hopefully they add achievements one day. However since there is no end goal in the game. You can get to level 500 with many hours needed to get there just to die and lose it all.
super awesome, super fun, and especially great value!
I hate robe boy, he FREAKING sucks. But I like picking up that dumb baby and throwing him around. Also I quit the game Everytime I get The Loom.

R.E.P.O. is a really fun game. Playing proximity chat with your friends with hilarious character designs and actions, tagged along with dark and creepy maps and scary creatures is a combination that will keep you gasping for air from all the laughter you will experience.

This game gets updated often and each update adds much needed content that you may not even have thought of previously that keeps the game interesting and inducing you to play with every new addition to the game--seeing all of the clips online, I couldn't help but purchase the game myself to get a piece of it and I say money well spent.

I highly recommend this game if you have a group of friends that you would want to experience something new, chaotic, and exciting with, or if you're solo and looking for an inexpensive and challenging game which keeps you moving and on your toes.

Thank you for reading, I hope I helped you with you with your decision, and I hope you have a great rest of your day.
The game is great I played 20 hours and whit friend is one of most fun game to play.I recommend.

Amazing game, especially since the creator made it amazingly well for friends, the upgrades too are amazing, i specificly love the new cosmetic update, overall 10/10 (my pc is horrible as well just wanted to mention that, my pc parts are all from 2014 yet runs R.E.P.O. perfectly on lowest graphics of course, but if i can run this game so can you)
Puto juego como lo amo. Valen verga todos y me rompen las cosas pero igual ta chido.
Super fun game, collect or something I don’t really know

This is an absolutely amazing game made by a wonderful team, but do be warned it can be difficult (that's part of the fun). Another great thing about this game is I currently have a pretty bad computer and as long as you mess with minimal settings it runs PERFECTLY! There is only 1 problem, unfortunately you cannot successfully propose to and marry the Robe to be your wife :(

This game is very fun :) the creators did very good with making this game scary and fun. i love playing it with my friends and it is very funny. we can be collecting items and trying not to break them, or being chased by monsters and screaming our lungs out. very fun game
really fun game i recommend not playing as much public games as much as playing with friends due to trollers and anoying people
This game is legendary. I've had so much fun. Even if you're having a hard time playing it, you can mod it super easily.
Very good game, sort of got addicted as soon as the cosmetic drop happened but do recommend to anyone
its a great game its loads of fun!!!! you can spend hours getting scared XD

If you're looking for a few jump scares with a guaranteed laugh from you and your friends. I highly recommend this game. 10/10 across the board. If you do play though, add me. I'm always looking for new people to play with.

I've always loved physics based games, I grew up playing Interactive Buddy, Grow Home, and pining for the day I could get my hands on Goat Simulator (though that day never came...), so REPO has always had its charm cast over me; even when I was stuck on 2 hours played for like a fiscal tax year. I have gotten back into it as of March or April, and by the time of writing this review I'm absolutely enchanted.
I couldn't ask for more when it comes to this game. Most common issues I encounter when playing "friendslop" games (for lack of a better term) are entirely absent here. It performs well on lower-end devices, the developers listen to and love their community, and communicate about upcoming changes very well. I adore the direction this game is going and only hope that the developers continue to work with their community towards a fun full release.

(edit for grammar)
good game it is really chaotic the walkie takies are the BEST.
One of the greats in terms of party games. Super casual fun, with great updates time and time again.

Incredibly fun game with friends! Love the worldbuilding, the little semi-bots are adorable and the devs are great (watch their dev videos on YT, honestly the best). The customizing feature added in 0.4 has made me jump into the game solo as well, and even if I am the absolute worst at surviving in this game, I'm having a lot of fun just roaming the first few levels looking for RAM boxes~
the cosmetic update is amazing, reached level 20 for the first time last night with friends and retrieved a legendary head piece. SO MUCH CUSTOMIZATION, SO MANY NEW ITEMS, I LOVE THIS GAME!

I played R.E.P.O. with my family, and it was absolutely fun and chaotic. I honestly can’t count how many times I was crying from laughing so hard. The game creates so many random and hilarious moments that every session feels different. It’s a really great game to relax, chill, and just have fun with friends or family.
It is very fun with friends. Less fun by yourself but top 5 game.

I got into a 21+ public game then one player said they were live on chaturbate so I checked and they weren't lying.

Immediate 10/10, reliable source for goon material.

holy crap, i got this game thinking i would be terrofied. i was, but playing this with friends is abousolute cinema. this game is a must have.



also made me hyper gay
It has this spooky vibe but its also very funny at the same time. Best to play with friends in my opinion and it also updates every know and then.

This game is so much fun, it's hilarious! I hope they add more stuff like maps, achievements and a merch shop, I would totally buy a repo shirt.

10/10

Decently well balanced good fun. All of the monsters are fun and well designed. The downsides are when you die, you do absolutely nothing but wait until you get revived and having to try and split the money made between players for upgrades and tools. There are plenty of good mods to make things more fun.

REPO may look pretty simple and doesn’t have many built-in maps, but the huge variety of mods keeps the game fresh and surprisingly replayable. It honestly brought back that fun feeling of playing chaotic co-op games with friends again. And the goofy facial expressions reacting to everyone’s mic input is the soul of the game. Watching friends carry around the valuables but end up breaking them is hilarious as well. Sometimes it’s more fun when everything goes wrong than when you finish the objective
Made the game impossible for casual players to get past the 3rd round. Monsters spawning non stop, and will attack you even when hidden and not making a sound.

Trash game. Got to level 10 and died because every single monster camped 2 rooms in front of truck. What the ♥♥♥♥ kind of mechanic is that? Oh, and fix the ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ timeout threshold. My network has a seizure for a second or some other random crash happens and i get kicked and me and my friends have to restart the whole session again? Make a way to be able to join an existing session. Like such basic stuff needs to get fix but hey, here is the cosmetics update! Read the ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ negative reviews and fix the stuff people tell you to fix. Stop exploiting the 'early access' tag.

Not fun anymore.

The last few patches have all increased the difficulty and according to the community it's now either a tryhard or cheating kind of game, no longer a fun co-op experience.  For the first part, people in discussions obviously say "git gud lol" and the latter, they tell you which mods you can download to make the game playable, because it's still perfectly balanced, just... you need to mod it so you're not playing with the intended balance.  Examples were "difficulty smoothing" and "shared upgrades"

You need to actually fully beat a level beyond the first to *potentially* unlock any cosmetics, they're also gated behind being a large bulky object to drag to an extraction - they can also take damage - and it's no easy feat to get past even the first stage anymore in a state where you'll be able to get past the second.  I've not done one with fewer than 2 monsters that appear fairly early, and generally there are 3 or more, which is just not good.  Several times, they've been in the very first room we walk into.

There are already built in mechanics that make the game considerably harder the longer you play so I don't see the need to make the early game as difficult as it is, with the increased amount of enemies and their level of aggression.  Multiple times enemies have behaved in ways that they didn't in previous versions.

Oh, and the shop items have been nerfed.

I have mixed feelings on the shop items, weapons especially. It really feels like the game is designed for you to fight instead of stealth at a point, now more than ever.  But that turns the game into a completely different experience for better or worse.  If you can make it to that point and manage to actually see them for sale, that is.

I will be checking back and may update my review because I have fond memories but I think the game is going in the wrong direction and has been for the last few patches.  Add a challenge mode, hard difficulty, whatever for people who want to play the game that way but leave the base experience as an accessible and casual party game where you can mess around with friends and still do something.  If you make a mistake and lose a bunch of stuff due to your own messups and gradually feel the attrition as you go through levels, that's perfectly fine.  But bad RNG where you get little loot in a level full of enemies that destroy it.. is not fun.  And this seems to be happening more and more this version.
I extremely like repo, very fun and is a funny game.
Client timeout every 20 minutes and people have been having this issue for years so im refunding might buy again if devs fix it :)

I'm not sure how this is overwhelmingly positive.  I play single player.  I don't mind fiddling around with things to figure out how they work.  With a lack of tutorial, I died about five times just trying to figure out what I was supposed to do.  The default graphics are horrible and just make the game hard to look at.  I finally had to consult google to find out how to activate the van.  I don't mind monsters, but when you have no defense or offense ability, trying to live long enough to finish even the first level is no fun.  Really... it's not enjoyable.  I don't have time in my life to keep repeating level 1 12 times because the game design is so overpowered to the negative.

Understanding that this is early access, I hope the developers take single users into account.
For someone who hates horror games, this is a fun and goofy game to still be jump-scared by.
Fun game and i realy like the regular updates. One thing that would lift the game is to include straps for the carts.
A Polished and comedic fun horror game so many fun experiences defo recommend it. just beware of people flinging you

After 100 hours sadly I have to leave a negative review. Just because after so long, the game is still so unbalanced in singleplayer, all the updates are being developed with multiplayer in mind, even with the "big" last cosmetic update. So if you don't play with friends, this game is not worth to play. The way you not can't pause, but lost the run you can spend days building in a single random event where you get a loom and a huntsman spawned, or some oogly detect you even if you hide, or if a random orb decides to explode throwing you? it's so frustrating.
The game is not about horror anymore, it's just having luck in the RNG to not get killed by some bs, skill is not even involved.
The only way to play this game in single game is modding it a lot, almost to make it a different game.

R.E.P.O is what happens when you give a bunch of underqualified goblins a repossession license and access to physics-based chaos.

Every mission starts with:
“Don't break anything.”

And ends with:

- someone getting chased by a spin wheel tortellini,
- a priceless vase ricocheting down three flights of stairs,
- one teammate screaming “MY BAD” while dragging a massive traffic light that explodes near all the loot,
- and the entire squad dying because somebody sneezed near a giant floating head.

10/10 experience.

The monsters are terrifying, but not nearly as terrifying as your friend confidently saying:
“Trust me, I know what I’m doing.”

You do not.

Nobody does.

And now they added cosmetic updates, which means I can finally look like a true trash gremlin while causing catastrophic property damage. Also the little bumper carts? Absolute peak game design. There's nothing better than running over teammates while Tokyo drifting directly into monsters. 

R.E.P.O isn’t really a horror game. It’s a cooperative insurance fraud simulator powered by panic, bad decisions, and the collective IQ of a microwave burrito.

REPO is the most amazing game I have ever seen! Its lethal company, but more fun and stupid! I love how we get to play online, but one problem, i want to see EVERY SERVER AVAILABLE, not just some in my region, but overall, IT IS THE MOST FUN TAX COLLECTION GAME YOU CAN EVER PLAY!!!
The game desperately needs alternative or more entertaining game modes to break the repetitive gameplay loop.
Games is extremely fun for the price. Best to have friends to play with but can be done solo

Those were the last words spoken during the entire session before we all got eaten by the Headman.

10/10 game

I’m having a really fun time with this game. Every run hits that dopamine rush where you just want to clear it faster and faster. At the start, the monsters are actually scary, but after a while… yeah, you kinda become the monster.

The mod scene is great too, tons of stuff from useful QoL to completely dumb (in a good way) mods. My favorite is “Level Scaling” since you can tweak pretty much everything, extractions, loot, monsters, even map size. And somehow it still runs super stable even with 100+ mods.

Big respect to the Semiwork team, they feel really involved with the community. Definitely worth checking their YouTube. Can’t wait to see what they add next, especially new maps.
Super fun to play with others, More fun if you have graphics settings turn up and not having lag
Although I've only played this game very little, it's super fun with friends. And I hope at some point it'll be on console so other friends can play as well.
I love this game so much im sad it died off like every other co-op extraction horror game

Lowkey one of the best games I've played. The Mcjannek(?) The station map is quite literally the death of half my runs, but we ball. Playing single player is still fun however, when you do stupid things with friends, it makes the game so funny and super enjoyable. 107 hours is INSANE...

Semiwork are absolute chads for keeping this game updated, while also making hilarious dev logs/videos announcing the updates.

Tons of fun both solo and with friends, would recommend.
